info.name: CareEZ Clinical AI API
info.description:
Complete collection for CareEZ public REST endpoints — IDDSI food texture classification (text, image, audio), aspiration risk screening, batch meal validation, and clinical Q&A RAG.

Base URL: `{{base_url}}`
Production server: https://www.seniordeli.com

**No authentication required.** All endpoints respond with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`.

⚠️ Clinical Disclaimer: Prototype rule-based classifiers — outputs are decision-support tools only. Clinical decisions require assessment by a qualified Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP).

Built by our parent social enterprise (Hong Kong SED-registered social enterprise).
hello@careez.org | https://careez.org

variable.description: Production base URL. Change to http://localhost:PORT for local dev.

exec: // Sets common headers that apply to all requests in this collection.
// Override at the request level if needed.
// Log the target URL for debugging
// These run after every request in the collection.

item.name: IDDSI Classification
item.description: Classify food or liquid texture level using the IDDSI framework (Levels 0–7).

exec: // No additional pre-request logic needed — collection level sets Origin.

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-classify**

Classify a food or liquid item from a plain-text description. Returns:
- `iddsi_level` (0–7)
- `label` — English IDDSI label
- `confidence` (0.0–1.0)
- `explanation_en` — English explanation
- `explanation_zh_hant` — Traditional Chinese explanation
- `multilingual_explanation` — all supported languages
- `warning` — clinical disclaimer

**Supported languages:** `en`, `zh-HK`, `tl`, `id`, `vi`, `th`, `es`

This example uses English with a congee description → expects IDDSI Level 4 (Extremely Thick / Puréed).

response.name: 200 OK — Congee → IDDSI Level 4
response.body:
{
  "iddsi_level": 4,
  "label": "Extremely Thick / Puréed",
  "confidence": 0.87,
  "explanation_en": "This food item matches IDDSI Level 4 (Extremely Thick / Puréed). It can be eaten with a spoon and does not require chewing.",
  "explanation_zh_hant": "此食物符合IDDSI第4級（極稠/糊狀）。可用匙羹進食，無需咀嚼。",
  "multilingual_explanation": "zh-Hant",
  "warning": "Prototype rule-based classifier — clinical decisions require qualified SLP"
}

item.name: 02 · Text → IDDSI Level (Traditional Chinese 繁體中文)

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-classify — Traditional Chinese input**

Same endpoint, Traditional Chinese food description (minced pork with sauce). Set `language: "zh-HK"` to receive explanations in Traditional Chinese.

Expected: IDDSI Level 5 (Minced & Moist).

item.name: 03 · Text → IDDSI Level (Simplified Chinese 简体中文)

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-classify — Simplified Chinese input**

Silken tofu description in Simplified Chinese → expects IDDSI Level 4 (Extremely Thick / Puréed).

exec: // For actual use: attach a real JPEG/PNG/WebP image file via the 'image' form field.
// This pre-request script logs a reminder.
console.log('[CareEZ] Attach a JPEG/PNG/WebP food image (max 10 MB) to the "image" form-data field.');
// Accept 200 (successful classification) or 400 (no file attached in test environment)

formdata.description: JPEG, PNG, or WebP image of the food/liquid item. Max 10 MB. For liquids, use a side-view photo in a transparent container.
Response language: en | zh-HK | tl | id | vi | th | es

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-classify-image**

Upload a food/liquid photo for visual IDDSI classification. Uses multipart/form-data.

**Fields:**
- `image` (required) — JPEG, PNG, or WebP file, max 10 MB
- `language` (optional) — response language; default `en`

**Tips:**
- For liquids: photograph in a transparent container from the side
- Visual accuracy is lower for liquids than solid foods
- Complement with a flow/viscosity test where possible

**Responses:** 200 (classified), 400 (invalid/missing image), 413 (>10 MB), 500 (server error)

response.name: 200 OK — Pureed soup → IDDSI Level 4
response.body:
{
  "iddsi_level": 4,
  "label": "Extremely Thick / Puréed",
  "confidence": 0.79,
  "explanation_en": "Visual analysis indicates a smooth, homogeneous purée consistent with IDDSI Level 4. No visible lumps or particulates detected.",
  "explanation_zh_hant": "視覺分析顯示質地均勻，符合IDDSI第4級（糊狀）。未見可見顆粒。",
  "multilingual_explanation": "zh-Hant",
  "warning": "Prototype rule-based classifier — clinical decisions require qualified SLP"
}

item.name: Aspiration Screening
item.description: Screen for aspiration risk via symptom description or (research-staged) voice/audio analysis.

request.description:
**POST /api/voice-aspiration-screen**

Screen for aspiration risk based on reported swallowing symptoms. Returns:
- `risk_level` — `low` | `moderate` | `high`
- `risk_score` — numeric 0–100 (higher = greater risk)
- `flags` — specific symptom flags identified
- `recommendation_en` — English clinical recommendation
- `recommendation_zh_hant` — Traditional Chinese recommendation
- `warning` — clinical disclaimer

Optional fields `age` and `diagnosis` improve risk stratification accuracy.

This example: 82yo stroke patient with wet voice + coughing + weight loss → expects HIGH risk.

response.name: 200 OK — High aspiration risk
response.body:
{
  "risk_level": "high",
  "risk_score": 78,
  "flags": ["wet/gurgly voice", "coughing during meals", "significant weight loss"],
  "recommendation_en": "High aspiration risk detected. Refer to a Speech-Language Pathologist for formal swallowing assessment immediately. Consider NPO (nil per os) pending evaluation.",
  "recommendation_zh_hant": "發現高度誤嚥風險。請立即轉介言語治療師進行正式吞嚥評估。評估前考慮禁食（NPO）。",
  "warning": "Prototype rule-based classifier — clinical decisions require qualified SLP"
}

request.description:
**POST /api/voice-aspiration-screen — Filipino/Tagalog input**

Same endpoint with Filipino symptom description. Language code `tl` (Tagalog). Useful for Filipino caregiver staff in RCHE settings.

Translation: 'The patient coughs while eating, has a wet/hoarse voice after swallowing, and has lost 2kg in the past month.'

formdata.description: WAV or MP3 audio of swallowing/voice. Max 30 MB. (Stub — returns 501)

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-classify-audio — RESEARCH-STAGED**

⚠️ This endpoint is not yet in production. It returns `501 Not Implemented` in all environments.

Planned capability: analyse swallowing/voice audio recordings to infer IDDSI-relevant swallowing function parameters. Ship timing coordinated with the audio analysis research agent.

**Do not use in clinical workflows.**

Included in collection for API design continuity and partner coordination.

response.name: 501 Not Implemented (expected)

item.name: Meal Validation
item.description: Validate full RCHE meal plans against a resident's prescribed IDDSI level using Claude Haiku.

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-batch**

Validate a full RCHE meal plan against a resident's prescribed IDDSI level. Uses Claude Haiku for per-item classification.

**Request fields:**
- `meal` — array of `{item, portion}` objects (max 20 items)
- `resident_iddsi_level` — prescribed level 0–7
- `language` — `en` | `zh-HK` | `zh-Hant` | `zh-Hans` | `tl` | `id`

**Response:**
- `items[]` — per-item classification with level, label, confidence, rationale
- `meal_safety_verdict` — `safe` | `caution` | `unsafe`
- `non_matching_items[]` — items exceeding resident's level
- `recommendations` — modification instructions
- `model` — Claude model used

**Note:** 30-second timeout. Returns 503 if Anthropic API key not configured.

response.name: 200 OK — 4-item meal, Level 5 resident, verdict: unsafe
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "item": "steamed fish",
      "portion": "100g",
      "iddsi_level": 6,
      "label": "Soft & Bite-Sized",
      "label_zh": "軟爛切粒",
      "color": "#64B4E1",
      "match_resident_level": false,
      "confidence": "high",
      "rationale": "Steamed fish is typically soft and easily flaked — Level 6 Soft & Bite-Sized."
    },
    {
      "item": "rice porridge (congee)",
      "portion": "200ml",
      "iddsi_level": 4,
      "label": "Pureed/Extremely Thick",
      "label_zh": "糊狀／極稠",
      "color": "#E06027",
      "match_resident_level": true,
      "confidence": "high",
      "rationale": "Congee is smooth and lump-free — Level 4 Pureed."
    }
  ],
  "meal_safety_verdict": "unsafe",
  "non_matching_items": [
    { "item": "steamed fish", "iddsi_level": 6, "label": "Soft & Bite-Sized" }
  ],
  "recommendations": "The following items require modification to meet the resident's Minced & Moist (Level 5) requirement:\n• steamed fish (currently IDDSI Level 6 – Soft & Bite-Sized): Mince finely and ensure moistened with sauce or gravy",
  "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  "resident_iddsi_level": 5,
  "resident_iddsi_label": "Minced & Moist",
  "disclaimer": "This meal assessment is for assistive reference only. Clinical decisions require a qualified SLP."
}

item.name: 09 · IDDSI Batch Meal Validation (RCHE — Traditional Chinese 繁體中文)

request.description:
**POST /api/iddsi-batch — Traditional Chinese (zh-HK)**

Same batch meal validation with Traditional Chinese food names and `language: "zh-HK"`. Rationale and recommendations will be returned in Traditional Chinese.

Useful for Hong Kong RCHE staff who use Chinese in documentation.

item.name: Clinical Q&A (RAG)
item.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation endpoint for dysphagia/IDDSI clinical questions. Searches 120-article softmeal.org knowledge base.

request.description:
**GET /api/clinical-qa**

Health check for the Clinical Q&A RAG endpoint. Returns:
- `status` — `ok`
- `index_size` — number of indexed softmeal.org articles (typically 120)
- `embedding_model` — `text-embedding-3-small`
- `answer_model` — `claude-haiku-4-5`
- `description` — endpoint description

Call this before using the POST endpoint to verify the RAG index is loaded.

response.name: 200 OK — Endpoint healthy
response.body:
{
  "status": "ok",
  "index_size": 120,
  "embedding_model": "text-embedding-3-small",
  "answer_model": "claude-haiku-4-5",
  "description": "CareEZ Clinical Q&A RAG — 120 softmeal.org articles across en/zh-HK/zh-CN/ja locales"
}

request.description:
**POST /api/clinical-qa**

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) endpoint that answers clinical questions about:
- Dysphagia management
- IDDSI texture levels
- 護食標準 (softmeal standards)

Searches a 120-article softmeal.org knowledge base.

**Request fields:**
- `question` (required) — 3–1000 characters, any supported language
- `language` (optional) — `en` | `zh-HK` | `zh-CN` | `ja` — boosts locale-matching results
- `top_k` (optional) — articles to retrieve (1–10, default 5)

**Response:**
- `answer` — AI-synthesised clinical answer
- `sources[]` — retrieved softmeal.org articles with title + URL
- `model` — Claude model used
- `embedding_model` — embedding model used
- `disclaimer` — clinical disclaimer in requested language

item.name: 10c · Clinical Q&A — Ask (POST, Cantonese 廣東話)

request.description:
**POST /api/clinical-qa — Cantonese (zh-HK)**

Same RAG endpoint with a question in Cantonese. `language: "zh-HK"` boosts locale-matching articles from the softmeal.org knowledge base.

Translation: 'What are the characteristics of IDDSI Level 4 foods? What type of patients is it suitable for?'
